notes: Fixes batch publication so each rendered item is copied and finalized independently, while retaining staging on publication failure.

updates.notes: Fixes batch publication so each rendered item is copied and finalized independently, while retaining staging on publication failure.

Preserves completed batch renders after post-render URI failures, adds durable artifact recovery, collision-safe staging, direct media verification, separate compilation sharing roots, and MediaStore publication.

Quick Mode keeps its fast 384px five-minute probe, but any uncertainty now creates fresh 640px decoder/OCR lanes for the complete 30-second fallback and exact-boundary refinement instead of reusing low-resolution evidence.

Experimental Quick Mode uses five-minute monotonic checkpoints, adaptive skipped-state recovery, 384px ROI-focused frame decoding, two or three independent decoder/OCR refinement lanes based on CPU count, severe-thermal fallback to one lane, 30-second scan fallback on uncertainty, audio transmuxing, and Media3 trim optimization.

Select multiple videos once; the app compiles them one at a time to keep decoder, OCR, encoder, memory, storage, and thermal load stable. The queue shows per-video state and elapsed time plus total batch elapsed time, and resumes its status after reopening.

Uses three-minute macro checkpoints only for persistent 1-to-N counters, recursively recovers skipped observed states, requires exact persistence for every boundary, and reruns the 30-second profile on any uncertainty.

Checks every five minutes while visible or in PiP and every 15 minutes in the background, verifies the release hash/package/version/signer, defers downloads during active compilation, and stops boundary OCR as soon as persistence is proven.

Adds compact auto-enter PiP, live percentage and exact status, optional rapid core telemetry, foreground CPU continuity, and lower progress-publication overhead. Detection and timestamp decisions are unchanged.
Latest tester build; includes v0.17.26 finite-report and confirmed-candidate deduplication fixes.
Initial integrated prototype based on CompilationMaker 0.17.25.
